summary: Backend robustness fix with no end-user UI; affected human is the VibePro operator reading gate evidence. No operator-facing regression: object shapes and CLI/gate output formats unchanged, no diagnostic removed. safeReaddir's ENOTDIR silent-skip is consistent with the pre-existing ENOENT silent-skip on the same function and yields the conservative 'not merged' result for merged-story aggregation, so silence is the right choice rather than a new warning. The buildEvidenceItem precedence fix is a usability improvement: evidence kind/ref a human reads is now protected from silent identity corruption. GER-S-001 and GER-S-003 fail against pre-fix behavior, genuinely gating the fix.

inspection.summary: Read the full diff of both files; read the three safeReaddir call sites (execution-state.js 780-818) confirming silent-skip is safe/conservative for merged-story aggregation; grepped all 16 buildEvidenceItem call sites; ran node --test gate-evidence-edge-robustness (5/5 pass).

judgment_delta: Initially concerned the new ENOTDIR silent-skip could hide a corrupt workspace from the operator (needs_changes candidate). Concluded pass: it mirrors the sibling ENOENT branch's existing silent contract and yields the conservative 'not merged' result, so a warning would add inconsistency rather than value; buildEvidenceItem strictly improves trustworthiness with no shape/format regression.
